clusters.title: Who did the Oscars 2026 reward and honour?

items.title: Who did the Oscars 2026 reward and honour?
items.standfirst: The politically charged action comedy “One Battle After Another” just managed to outmuscle Ryan Coogler’s musically driven vampire thriller “Sinners." in the 2026 Oscars Ceremony. But Michael B. Jordan still won best actor for “Sinners” and Jessie Buckley won for “Hamnet,” making her the first Irish performer to ever win in the category.
items.content: The politically charged action comedy “One Battle After Another” just managed to outmuscle Ryan Coogler’s musically driven vampire thriller “Sinners." in the 2026 Oscars Ceremony. But Michael B. Jordan still won best actor for “Sinners” and Jessie Buckley won for “Hamnet,” making her the first Irish performer to ever win in the category.

items.title: Michael B. Jordan gets 'most thunderous applause of the night' at Oscars
items.standfirst: Michael B. Jordan has been building toward a performance like “Sinners” for over 20 years. Now he has the best actor Oscar as his reward in what proved to be a hugely popular win. The Oscars also rewarded Paul Thomas Anderson's 'One Battle After Another' with six prizes, although it wasn't a clean sweep. FRANCE 24's Wassim Cornet tells us more from Los Angeles
items.content: Michael B. Jordan has been building toward a performance like “Sinners” for over 20 years. Now he has the best actor Oscar as his reward in what proved to be a hugely popular win. The Oscars also rewarded Paul Thomas Anderson's 'One Battle After Another' with six prizes, although it wasn't a clean sweep. FRANCE 24's Wassim Cornet tells us more from Los Angeles

items.title: 'One Battle of Another' wins big at Oscars 2026 as Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley rewarded
items.standfirst: Paul Thomas Anderson’s 'One Battle After Another' was crowned best picture at the 98th Academy Awards, handing Hollywood’s top honor to a comic, multi-generational American saga of political resistance. Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley also won awards for best actors. For FRANCE 24's Ben Crowe, Paul Thomas Anderson's blockbuster's huge win is no surprise.
items.content: Paul Thomas Anderson’s 'One Battle After Another' was crowned best picture at the 98th Academy Awards, handing Hollywood’s top honor to a comic, multi-generational American saga of political resistance. Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley also won awards for best actors. For FRANCE 24's Ben Crowe, Paul Thomas Anderson's blockbuster's huge win is no surprise.

items.standfirst: Paul Thomas Anderson's political chase film earned six Oscars, beating the vampire thriller Sinners, which scored four.

items.content: "One Battle After Another" took home several big awards, including Best Picture, Best Director and the newly created Best Casting.

items.title: 'One Battle After Another' wins six Oscars including best picture
items.standfirst: Paul Thomas Anderson’s “One Battle After Another” won best picture at the 98th Academy Awards on Sunday, capping a long-awaited Oscars breakthrough for the acclaimed filmmaker. The film won six awards overall, while Michael B. Jordan took best actor and cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history with her win.
items.content: Paul Thomas Anderson’s “One Battle After Another” won best picture at the 98th Academy Awards on Sunday, capping a long-awaited Oscars breakthrough for the acclaimed filmmaker. The film won six awards overall, while Michael B. Jordan took best actor and cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history with her win.

items.title: 🔴 'One Battle After Another' wins Oscar for best picture
'One Battle After Another' takes best picture. Here's the full list of Oscar winners
items.standfirst: Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley won best actor and best actress. Paul Thomas Anderson received best director. Cassandra Kulukundis won the Academy's first ever casting award.
items.content: One Battle After Another accept the Academy Award for best picture at the 2026 Oscars.'/>Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley won best actor and best actress. Paul Thomas Anderson received best director. Cassandra Kulukundis won the Academy's first ever casting award.(Image credit: Kevin Winter)

clusters.title: Trump calls for help on securing Strait of Hormuz as oil prices surge
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump Seeks Help For Strait Of Hormuz

items.title: Trump calls for help on securing Strait of Hormuz as oil prices surge
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump is turning to other countries for help policing the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closed by Iran for the US and its allies after the US and Israel launched the war in Tehran. Since the closing of the crucial passageway, through which one fifth the world's traded oil normally flows, oil prices have surged significantly.
items.content: US President Donald Trump is turning to other countries for help policing the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closed by Iran for the US and its allies after the US and Israel launched the war in Tehran. Since the closing of the crucial passageway, through which one fifth the world's traded oil normally flows, oil prices have surged significantly.

clusters.title: Israel expands its ground operation in southern Lebanon
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel Expands Ground Operation In Lebanon

items.title: Israel expands its ground operation in southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: The Israeli army says it has begun limited targeted ground operations against Hezbollah in South Lebanon. It comes three weeks after the US and Israel began carrying out strikes in Iran, a war that has since escalated throughout the Middle East, and beyond. Since the start of the war, over 800,000 people have been displaced and at least 800 killed.
items.content: The Israeli army says it has begun limited targeted ground operations against Hezbollah in South Lebanon. It comes three weeks after the US and Israel began carrying out strikes in Iran, a war that has since escalated throughout the Middle East, and beyond. Since the start of the war, over 800,000 people have been displaced and at least 800 killed.

items.title: IDF launches 'limited operation' in Lebanon as 'buffer zone expands significantly'
items.standfirst: The Israeli military said on March 16 it had begun what it described as "limited ground operations" against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.The announcement echoes similar statements issued in 2024, when Israel and Hezbollah fought a major war in Lebanon, and in 2023, when the military launched a ground assault in Gaza in response to Hamas's October 7 attacks. "Yet the buffer zone is expanding significantly", says FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky from Jerusalem.
items.content: The Israeli military said on March 16 it had begun what it described as "limited ground operations" against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.The announcement echoes similar statements issued in 2024, when Israel and Hezbollah fought a major war in Lebanon, and in 2023, when the military launched a ground assault in Gaza in response to Hamas's October 7 attacks. "Yet the buffer zone is expanding significantly", says FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky from Jerusalem.

items.standfirst: Israeli army says its forces are targeting key locations in southern Lebanon, aiming to expand forward defence zones.
items.content: Israeli army says its forces are targeting key locations in southern Lebanon, aiming to expand forward defence zones.

clusters.title: European takeover battle hots up with UniCredit’s ‘unfriendly attack’ on Commerzbank

items.title: European takeover battle hots up with UniCredit’s ‘unfriendly attack’ on Commerzbank
items.standfirst: Milan-based bank plans to up its near-30% stake in German lender to trigger formal talks despite strong opposition from BerlinTwo European banking powerhouses have become embroiled in a €35bn (£30bn) takeover battle after Italy’s UniCredit stepped up its long-running pursuit of German lender Commerzbank, despite strong opposition from the German government.UniCredit first took a stake of 9% in Commerzbank in September 2024 and has since built up its holding to just under 30%. It said on Monday it was pushing to increase that holding further and push the rival lender into formal merger talks. Continue reading...
items.content: Milan-based bank plans to up its near-30% stake in German lender to trigger formal talks despite strong opposition from BerlinTwo European banking powerhouses have become embroiled in a €35bn (£30bn) takeover battle after Italy’s UniCredit stepped up its long-running pursuit of German lender Commerzbank, despite strong opposition from the German government.UniCredit first took a stake of 9% in Commerzbank in September 2024 and has since built up its holding to just under 30%. It said on Monday it was pushing to increase that holding further and push the rival lender into formal merger talks. Continue reading...

items.title: FCC chair threatens to throttle news broadcasts over ‘hoaxes’ about Iran war
items.standfirst: Brendan Carr posts that he may cancel spectrum permits of ‘mainstream news’ outlets for ‘misleading’ coverageThe Trump administration’s communications licensing tsar fired a warning shot over the US broadcasting industry Saturday, threatening to cancel the spectrum permits of broadcasters pushing what he termed “hoaxes and news distortions”.Federal Communications Commission (FCC) chair Brendan Carr posted on social media that broadcasters running “fake news – have a chance now to correct course before their license renewals come up. The law is clear. Broadcasters must operate in the public interest, and they will lose their licenses if they do not.” Continue reading...
items.content: Brendan Carr posts that he may cancel spectrum permits of ‘mainstream news’ outlets for ‘misleading’ coverageThe Trump administration’s communications licensing tsar fired a warning shot over the US broadcasting industry Saturday, threatening to cancel the spectrum permits of broadcasters pushing what he termed “hoaxes and news distortions”.Federal Communications Commission (FCC) chair Brendan Carr posted on social media that broadcasters running “fake news – have a chance now to correct course before their license renewals come up. The law is clear. Broadcasters must operate in the public interest, and they will lose their licenses if they do not.” Continue reading...
